persona.name: Shota Kimura

occupation.title: Medical Representative (MR)
occupation.organization: Daiichi Sankyo Co., Ltd., Tohoku Regional Sales Division
occupation.description: Visits hospitals and clinics across the Miyagi and Iwate prefectures to promote pharmaceutical products to physicians. Has 6-8 doctor appointments per day, each requiring different preparation and follow-up. Spends significant time driving between facilities, attending internal sales meetings, and filing visit reports. Required to document every doctor interaction for compliance.

persona.education: B.Pharm. from Tohoku University. Passed the MR certification exam in his first year at the company. Considered pharmacy practice but chose the higher-paying MR track.
long_term_goals: Get promoted to area manager within 3 years
Pay off his car loan and start saving seriously
Maintain his 'top 10% MR' ranking in the regional division
Eventually transfer to the marketing department at head office
persona.style: Clean-cut and energetic in a way that reads as earnest rather than slick. Always in a pressed suit with a company badge. Carries a tablet and a stack of product brochures. Speaks politely and quickly, adapting his communication style to each doctor — deferential with senior physicians, more casual with younger ones. Takes notes on his phone between appointments while sitting in his car. Uses LINE stickers generously with friends and colleagues. In team meetings he's enthusiastic but doesn't push back on his manager.

traits: Ambitious and competitive about sales rankings
Adaptable communicator — reads the room quickly
Diligent about compliance documentation even when it feels tedious
Sociable and good at building rapport with physicians
Follows instructions from management without much questioning
Impatient with slow internal systems but doesn't know what alternatives exist
Optimistic and action-oriented rather than strategic

interests: Pharmaceutical industry trends and new drug launches
Driving — knows every back road between Sendai and Morioka
Online gaming with high school friends on weekends
Fitness — goes to the gym 3 times a week
likes: Apps that save him time on paperwork between appointments
Simple tools he can use on his phone while in the car
When a doctor remembers him and his product pitch
Clear sales targets with visible progress tracking
Sendai's relaxed pace compared to Tokyo
Getting a new product approved that he can pitch to his doctors
dislikes: Spending 2 hours every evening writing visit reports
Internal meetings that repeat information he already knows
The company's clunky CRM system (SFA) that crashes on his tablet
Doctors who make him wait 45 minutes past the appointment time
Being out of the loop on tools that other MRs are using
Mandatory e-learning modules that have nothing to do with his daily work
Cold Sendai winters and icy driving conditions

beliefs: If a tool saves me 30 minutes a day, it's worth any reasonable price
Sales is ultimately about relationships, but technology should handle the busywork
Young MRs who adopt tools faster will outperform senior ones stuck in old systems
Good meeting notes are the difference between remembering a doctor's preferences and losing the next appointment
skills: Pharmaceutical product knowledge and clinical discussion

general: Voice-records key points from doctor meetings on his phone immediately after leaving
Types up visit reports in the car between appointments using the company tablet
Asks colleagues on LINE which tools they use for meeting prep
Checks his sales rankings dashboard obsessively

morning: Wakes at 6:30, quick gym session or stretching
Breakfast: onigiri and coffee from the convenience store
Reviews today's doctor appointment schedule in the car
workday: 30-45 minutes of driving between each appointment
Lunch in the car or at a roadside restaurant
Back at the branch office by 17:00 for team meeting
evening: 17:30-19:30: writes visit reports and prepares materials for tomorrow
Drives home, picks up dinner or cooks rice and a simple meal
Games online with friends or watches soccer highlights
weekend: Saturday: sleeps in, gym, then gyutan lunch with friends
Afternoon: errands or drives to the coast
Evening: prepares materials for Monday's appointments

health.physical: Fit from regular gym sessions. Sits in his car for hours daily, which gives him occasional lower back tightness. Eats irregularly — too many convenience store meals. His last health check was clean, but he knows his diet needs improvement.
health.mental: Generally upbeat and resilient. Competitive pressure creates occasional stress spikes around quarterly targets. Handles rejection from doctors well — 'part of the job.' Social connections through gaming and after-work dinners keep him grounded.

relationships.name: Yusuke Abe
relationships.description: 38, a veteran MR who leads by example. Shota respects him but finds his management style old-school — too many in-person meetings.

relationships.name: Haruki Suzuki
relationships.description: 27, covers the neighboring territory. They compare sales strategies and complain about the CRM system over protein shakes.

relationships.name: Akiko Kimura
relationships.description: 54, nurse at a clinic in Sendai. Lives 20 minutes away. Shota has dinner at her place once a week and she worries he's not eating enough vegetables.
